theme: Make Every Pixel Pull Its Weight — Context, Continuity, and Character

ideas.title: Desk Nameplates for Regular Workers — Own Your Desk
ideas.description: The manager desk has a `.mgr-desk__nameplate` div that says 'Manager', but the six regular desks are anonymous rectangles. Workers arrive, sit down, and vanish into the void with zero desk identity. Fix this by adding a `.desk__nameplate` element inside each desk div in Office.jsx, rendering only when the desk has a non-ghost occupant. Content: `worker.name.split(' ')[0]` in tiny monospace. Style in Office.css: `.desk__nameplate { position:absolute; bottom:-16px; left:50%; transform:translateX(-50%); font-size:0.6rem; font-weight:700; letter-spacing:0.06em; text-transform:uppercase; color:var(--text-muted); white-space:nowrap; pointer-events:none; }`. When `desk--active` is true, add `.desk__nameplate--active` class: `color:var(--role-color,var(--accent)); text-shadow:0 0 8px var(--role-color,var(--accent)); transition:color 0.4s, text-shadow 0.4s;`. For error desks add `.desk__nameplate--error { color:var(--red); animation:nameplate-pulse 0.8s ease-in-out infinite; }` `@keyframes nameplate-pulse { 0%,100% { opacity:1; } 50% { opacity:0.5; } }`. Ghost desks get no nameplate (keeps their eerie emptiness). WHY: The office floor is a spatial story — 'Maya' glowing teal under a hot desk makes the space feel occupied and real. Error nameplates pulsing red turn the floor into a monitoring instrument. This is one div, one CSS block, and pure atmosphere.

ideas.title: Error Badge on Dashboard Tab — The Tab Speaks
ideas.description: Currently when a worker hits error status, nothing changes in the tab bar — you have to already be on Dashboard to see it. Add a live error badge count directly to the '📊 Dashboard' tab button label. In App.jsx, compute `const errorCount = activeWorkers.filter(w => w.status === 'error').length`. In the Dashboard tab button JSX, after the label text add: `{errorCount > 0 && {errorCount}}`. CSS in App.css: `.tab-error-badge { display:inline-flex; align-items:center; justify-content:center; margin-left:6px; min-width:16px; height:16px; padding:0 4px; background:var(--red); color:#fff; font-size:0.65rem; font-weight:800; border-radius:99px; vertical-align:middle; animation:badge-pop 0.3s cubic-bezier(0.34,1.56,0.64,1); }` `@keyframes badge-pop { from { transform:scale(0); } to { transform:scale(1); } }`. When errorCount drops to 0, remove the badge (React condition handles this). Also update the document title prefix: the existing `⚠️` prefix is already there — no change needed. WHY: The whole point of the tab UI is to let you look away from dashboard. An error badge means users catch incidents without babysitting the tab. This is the feature that justifies having two tabs at all.

ideas.title: Ghost Worker 'OOO' Tooltip — The Empty Desk Has a Story
ideas.description:
Ghost workers (roster members not currently active) sit at desks as greyed-out figures with zero information — you can hover them but the `data-tooltip` attribute only shows their role. That is a wasted interaction. Upgrade ghosts with an OOO (out-of-office) hover speech bubble in Office.jsx. In the Character component, detect `variant === 'ghost'` and add hover state. `const [ghostBubble, setGhostBubble] = useState(false)`. On `.char--ghost` div: `onMouseEnter={() => setGhostBubble(true)} onMouseLeave={() => setGhostBubble(false)}`. Render inside the char div: `{variant === 'ghost' && ghostBubble && (
📵 OOO
{worker.role}
)}`. Style: `.ghost-ooo-bubble { position:absolute; bottom:calc(100% + 8px); left:50%; transform:translateX(-50%); background:rgba(30,27,75,0.92); border:1px solid rgba(139,92,246,0.3); border-radius:8px; padding:6px 10px; font-size:0.68rem; color:#c4b5fd; white-space:nowrap; pointer-events:none; z-index:150; animation:ooo-in 0.15s ease; text-align:center; line-height:1.5; }` `.ghost-ooo-role { font-size:0.62rem; color:#8b5cf6; display:block; }` `@keyframes ooo-in { from { opacity:0; transform:translateX(-50%) scale(0.9); } to { opacity:1; transform:translateX(-50%) scale(1); } }`. Also remove the existing `data-tooltip` prop from ghost Characters (tooltip and bubble would conflict). WHY: Empty desks are a story. Right now ghost Carlos is a grey smear. Ghost Carlos with 'OOO / Backend Engineer' bubble is a person who happens to be offline. One hover handler turns the floor from a chart into a team.

ideas.title: Vibe Change Cinematic Announcement — Your New Vibe Has Arrived
ideas.description:
The current vibe change experience: confetti fires if vibe improves (great), nothing happens if it worsens (tragic). Both directions deserve ceremony. Add a 2.5-second full-overlay vibe change announcement card. In App.jsx add `const [vibeAnnouncement, setVibeAnnouncement] = useState(null)`. In the vibe streak useEffect, when `prevKey !== null && prevKey !== teamVibe.key`, call `setVibeAnnouncement({ from: prevKey, to: teamVibe.key, label: teamVibe.label, emoji: teamVibe.emoji })`. Then `setTimeout(() => setVibeAnnouncement(null), 2500)`. Render as a portal at document.body or directly at the top of the app div: `{vibeAnnouncement && (
Vibe shift
{vibeAnnouncement.emoji}
{vibeAnnouncement.label}
)}`. CSS: `.vibe-announce { position:fixed; inset:0; z-index:1000; display:flex; align-items:center; justify-content:center; pointer-events:none; }` `.vibe-announce__card { background:var(--surface); border:1px solid var(--border); border-radius:20px; padding:2rem 3rem; text-align:center; box-shadow:0 24px 80px rgba(0,0,0,0.6); animation:vibe-announce-in 0.5s cubic-bezier(0.34,1.56,0.64,1) both, vibe-announce-out 0.4s ease 2s forwards; }` `@keyframes vibe-announce-in { from { opacity:0; transform:scale(0.7) translateY(30px); } to { opacity:1; transform:none; } }` `@keyframes vibe-announce-out { to { opacity:0; transform:scale(0.9) translateY(-20px); } }` `.vibe-announce__emoji { font-size:3rem; margin:0.5rem 0; }` `.vibe-announce__name { font-size:1.4rem; font-weight:800; color:var(--text); }` `.vibe-announce__label { font-size:0.75rem; font-weight:600; color:var(--text-muted); text-transform:uppercase; letter-spacing:0.1em; }`. The getTeamVibe utility already returns emoji — use it. Suppress during initial load (only fire when prevKey !== null). WHY: Vibe is the heartbeat of this dashboard. When it shifts, it should feel like a moment. The cinematic card takes 500ms to arrive and 2s to leave — long enough to register, short enough to not block work.

ideas.title: Desk Monitor SOS Morse Code on Error — The Desk Is Screaming
ideas.description: When a worker hits error status, `desk--error` adds a red tint to the desk. But the `.desk__monitor` (the little screen rectangle on each desk) does nothing special — it stays the same flat color. Error desks deserve a more visceral visual signal: SOS in morse code flashing on the monitor. In Office.css, add `.desk--error .desk__monitor { animation: sos-morse 4.2s step-start infinite; }` and define the animation with precise morse SOS timing (S=short short short, O=long long long): `@keyframes sos-morse { 0%   { background: var(--red); opacity:1; }` `5%   { opacity:0; }` `10%  { opacity:1; }` `15%  { opacity:0; }` `20%  { opacity:1; }` `25%  { opacity:0; }` `33%  { background: #ff6600; opacity:1; }` `40%  { opacity:0; }` `49%  { opacity:1; }` `56%  { opacity:0; }` `65%  { opacity:1; }` `70%  { opacity:0; }` `75%  { opacity:1; }` `80%  { opacity:0; }` `83%  { opacity:1; }` `88%  { opacity:0; }` `93%  { opacity:1; }` `98%  { opacity:0; }` `100% { opacity:1; } }`. The dash-dot pattern at CSS timing is approximate but recognizable. Pair it with a 2px red box-shadow pulse: `.desk--error .desk__monitor { box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px var(--red), 0 0 12px rgba(239,68,68,0.4); }`. This is a zero-JS CSS-only change. WHY: SOS is the universal distress signal. Engineers recognize the rhythm subconsciously even if they can't name it. A morse code monitor creates ambient urgency without a banner, without an alert, without noise — just light, spelling help.

ideas.title: Bloberto's Completion Corkboard — The Wins Wall
ideas.description: The office wall has a whiteboard for vibe on the left but nothing between the whiteboard and the manager desk. That dead space is the perfect location for a tiny corkboard that celebrates completions in real-time. Add a `.office-corkboard` div in Office.jsx with position inside the office floor (left:21%, top:3%), sized 72px wide × 48px tall. Background: a 4px-bordered rounded rect with `#8B4513`-family brown tone and subtle crosshatch pattern via CSS `background-image: repeating-linear-gradient(45deg, rgba(0,0,0,0.06) 0,rgba(0,0,0,0.06) 1px, transparent 0, transparent 50%) center/8px 8px`. Pass `completions` as a prop to Office: in App.jsx compute `const recentCompletions = activityLog.filter(e => e.type === 'complete').slice(-3)` and pass to Office. Inside Office, render up to 3 `.corkboard-note` children — one per completion. Each note: `position:absolute; width:18px; height:18px; border-radius:2px; font-size:0.42rem; line-height:1.2; padding:2px; overflow:hidden; transform:rotate({-3 to +3}deg)`. Assign a pastel background per note index (yellow, green, pink). On note mount add CSS `animation: note-pin 0.3s cubic-bezier(0.34,1.56,0.64,1)`. Show worker initial + task truncated to 12 chars. Add a tiny CSS circle pin at top center. `@keyframes note-pin { from { transform:scale(0) rotate(0deg); } to { transform:scale(1) rotate(var(--note-rot,2deg)); } }`. WHY: The office floor only reacts to failure (error glitches) and vibe. It never celebrates success. The corkboard puts wins on the wall literally and figuratively. Three sticky notes that appear as the team ships is the difference between a monitoring dashboard and a team culture artifact.

ideas.title: Smooth Progress Bar Interpolation — Bars That Breathe
ideas.description: Every 30 seconds when data syncs, progress bars snap — 47% instantly becomes 62%, no ceremony. This makes the dashboard feel mechanical. Add smooth animated transitions between progress states. In App.jsx add `const prevProgressRef = useRef({})` keyed by worker id. After `setAllWorkers` resolves and before render, snapshot current progresses: in the syncFromGitHub callback, after setting allWorkers compute and store previous progresses. Pass `prevProgress={prevProgressRef.current[worker.id] ?? worker.progress}` to each WorkerCard, then to ProgressBar as a prop. In ProgressBar, set the `.progress-bar-fill` initial width via inline style using prevProgress on first render, then trigger a CSS transition to the current progress value. Use a useEffect with a 16ms requestAnimationFrame delay: `useEffect(() => { const raf = requestAnimationFrame(() => { if (fillRef.current) fillRef.current.style.width = min(progress,100) + '%' }) return () => cancelAnimationFrame(raf) }, [progress])`. The CSS transition: `.progress-bar-fill { transition: width 0.8s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); }`. Remove the existing `width` inline style and move it to a ref approach. When delta > 20%, briefly flash the fill: add `.progress-bar-fill--leap` class for 600ms with `box-shadow: 0 0 8px var(--green)` and `filter: brightness(1.15)`. Update prevProgressRef after each sync. WHY: Smooth progress tells a story of work happening in real-time even when data is 30 seconds stale. The 0.8s ease-in-out is long enough to be noticed, short enough to feel real. The leap flash for big jumps rewards the eye and signals meaningful progress.

ideas.title: Longest Active Grinder Crown — Honor the Marathon Runner
ideas.description:
Currently all working workers look identical in terms of recognition. But someone who has been working for 4 continuous hours is invisible next to someone who started 5 minutes ago. Surface the marathon runner. In App.jsx, inside the useMemo for activeWorkers or in a separate derivation, compute: `const longestGrinderId = useMemo(() => { const working = activeWorkers.filter(w => w.status === 'working' && w.updated_at); if (working.length < 2) return null; return working.reduce((oldest, w) => new Date(w.updated_at) < new Date(oldest.updated_at) ? w : oldest).id; }, [activeWorkers])`. Pass `isLongestGrinder={worker.id === longestGrinderId}` to WorkerCard. In WorkerCard, when `isLongestGrinder`, add a `.grinder-crown` span next to the worker name: `{isLongestGrinder && 👑}`. CSS: `.grinder-crown { font-size:0.85rem; margin-left:5px; display:inline-block; animation:crown-bob 2s ease-in-out infinite; }` `@keyframes crown-bob { 0%,100% { transform:translateY(0) rotate(-5deg); } 50% { transform:translateY(-3px) rotate(5deg); } }`. Also in Office.jsx, pass the longestGrinderId down (or compute it there from workers) and add a `.char--marathon` class to that Character, which adds a tiny crown SVG element above the avatar: `{variant === 'working' && isMarathon &&
👑
}` positioned absolute top:-14px left:50% transform:-50%. WHY: Recognition is motivation. Surfacing the marathon runner costs two lines of computation and a CSS animation. But to the person who has been grinding since 8am, seeing their crown appear at the 10am sync is delightful. It is also useful to managers — who has been at it longest and might need a break?

ideas.title: Office Atmosphere Layer — The Floor Has a Mood
ideas.description:
The office floor is a flat static background. Meanwhile the vibe system — the single most expressive piece of data this dashboard has — does nothing to the environment itself beyond the whiteboard text and monitor tint. Add a full-size `.office-atmosphere` div as the first child of `.office-floor` with `position:absolute; inset:0; pointer-events:none; z-index:0; overflow:hidden; border-radius:inherit`. Per vibe, render different CSS-only animated children:

**crushing** — 12 tiny `.atmo-spark` spans: `position:absolute; width:3px; height:3px; border-radius:50%; background:#fbbf24; opacity:0; animation:spark-rise calc(2s + var(--d,0s)) ease-in infinite; left:calc(var(--x,50) * 1%); bottom:0; --d:calc(var(--i,0) * 0.3s)` `@keyframes spark-rise { 0% { transform:translateY(0) scale(1); opacity:0.8; } 100% { transform:translateY(-80px) scale(0); opacity:0; } }`

**on-fire** — `.atmo-heat` div full-width: `background:linear-gradient(transparent,rgba(239,68,68,0.04)); animation:heat-shimmer 1.2s ease-in-out infinite alternate` `@keyframes heat-shimmer { from { transform:scaleY(1); } to { transform:scaleY(1.008) translateY(-2px); } }`

**slow-day** — 8 `.atmo-mote` divs: `position:absolute; width:2px; height:2px; border-radius:50%; background:rgba(156,163,175,0.5); animation:mote-drift calc(8s + var(--d,0s)) ease-in-out infinite alternate; left:calc(var(--x,20)*1%); top:calc(var(--y,40)*1%); --d:calc(var(--i)*1.2s)`

**after-hours** — 6 `.atmo-star` spans: `position:absolute; width:2px; height:2px; border-radius:50%; background:#c4b5fd; opacity:0; animation:star-twinkle calc(2.5s + var(--d,0s)) ease-in-out infinite; left:calc(var(--x)*1%); top:calc(var(--y)*1%)`

Set hardcoded --x/--y/--i CSS custom properties per span via inline style. Apply conditionally via vibe prop already available in Office.jsx. WHY: The vibe system is the soul of the product. Right now it is expressed in words and colors. Atmosphere makes it spatial — you feel crushing, you feel slow-day. Pure CSS, zero layout impact, pointer-events:none so it never interrupts interaction. The difference between a dashboard and a world.

ideas.title: Footer Version String Easter Egg — v???.???-chaos
ideas.description: The footer reads: `v1.0.0-chaos` and it has never changed. It is the most underused character in the entire app. Add a click handler that cycles through 12 increasingly unhinged version strings, persisted in localStorage so it survives refreshes. In App.jsx add: `const VERSION_STRINGS = ['v1.0.0-chaos', 'v1.0.1-too-many-coffees', 'v2.0.0-friday-deploy', 'v3.14-pi-in-the-sky', 'v4.0.0-shipped-it', 'v4.2.0-bloberto-approved', 'v5.0.0-we-deleted-prod', 'v6.6.6-definitely-fine', 'v7.0.0-just-vibes', 'v8.0.0-carlos-was-right', 'v9.9.9-final-final-FINAL', 'v∞.0.0-we-never-stop']`. `const [versionIdx, setVersionIdx] = useState(() => parseInt(localStorage.getItem('bloberto-version-idx') || '0', 10))`. On click: `const nextIdx = (versionIdx + 1) % VERSION_STRINGS.length; setVersionIdx(nextIdx); localStorage.setItem('bloberto-version-idx', String(nextIdx))`. Render in footer: `{VERSION_STRINGS[versionIdx]}`. Add a brief bounce animation on click: add class `version-string--clicked` and remove after 300ms. CSS: `.version-string { color:var(--accent2); transition:color 0.15s; } .version-string:hover { color:var(--accent); } .version-string--clicked { animation:version-bounce 0.3s cubic-bezier(0.34,1.56,0.64,1); }` `@keyframes version-bounce { 0% { transform:scale(1); } 50% { transform:scale(1.25); } 100% { transform:scale(1); } }`. At v5.0.0-we-deleted-prod specifically, flash the entire footer background red for 400ms for extra drama. WHY: Easter eggs are the love language of developer tools. The footer is a throwaway line today. After this change, engineers who notice the version string will click it once, laugh, and click it 11 more times to see where it ends. v∞.0.0-we-never-stop is the canonical truth of every software project.
